verified_pdf_fields.court: United States District Court for the District of Columbia
verified_pdf_fields.title: Deputy Director for Operations, National Park Service
verified_pdf_fields.page_refs: Page 2 of 2, visible lines 34-38 in extracted text

verified_damage_language_summary: The PDF states that on June 9, 2026, after rehabilitation was substantially complete, U.S. Park Police responded to an NPS report of damage including caulk over foam sealant cut with a sharp knife or razor, destruction of delaminating surface material, and approximately 70 fence post tops thrown into the pool.

gate.reason: The source URL and PDF text are verified. The SHA-256 digest is bound as supplied by the user, but was not independently recomputed by the assistant runtime.

claim_effect.reason: The declaration strengthens the official intentional-cut subclaim but does not resolve broader scale, algae causation, coating failure, or photo/surveillance questions.

open_questions_preserved: Scale of deliberate cuts: localized damage versus larger gash claims.
Coating or renovation contribution to delamination/peeling.
Photo, surveillance, arrest-detail, and contractor-document hash binding.
Independent recomputation of submitted PDF SHA-256 by a local verifier.
